content:
UFS Study Offer — What It Is and What to Do

A UFS study offer (also called an offer of study) is the official acceptance letter from the University of the Free State (UFS / Kovsies), confirming that you have been provisionally or unconditionally accepted into a degree programme for the 2026 academic year.

If you searched for www.ufs.ac.za study offer, you are likely looking for how to access, accept, or understand your UFS offer of study — this guide covers all of that.

How to Access Your UFS Study Offer Online

  1. Go to www.ufs.ac.za — This is the official University of the Free State website.
  2. Click on "myUFS" or the student portal link — Found in the top navigation bar.
  3. Log in with your UFS applicant or student number and password.
  4. Navigate to "Application Status" — Your study offer will appear here once issued.
  5. Download your UFS study offer letter — Save a copy for your records.

🔗 Go to University of the Free State website → www.ufs.ac.za

Types of UFS Study Offers

Offer TypeMeaningWhat to Do
Conditional OfferAccepted subject to achieving your final matric resultsContinue studying hard — your offer is withdrawn if you don't meet the conditions
Unconditional OfferFully accepted — your matric results already meet the requirementsAccept the offer immediately and register
WaitlistedOn the waiting list — a place may open upContinue to monitor your application status; also apply elsewhere
UnsuccessfulNot accepted for this programmeConsider alternative programmes, campuses, or other universities

How to Accept Your UFS Study Offer

Once you receive your UFS study offer, you must formally accept it before the deadline stated in the letter. To accept:

  • Log in to the UFS student portal at www.ufs.ac.za
  • Navigate to your application status page
  • Click "Accept Offer" and follow the prompts
  • Pay the registration deposit (if required) to secure your place

⚠️ Important: If you do not accept your UFS study offer before the deadline, your place may be given to another applicant.

UFS Registration After Accepting Your Offer

After accepting your UFS study offer, the next step is registration. UFS typically holds registration in January for the first semester. Key registration information:

  • 📅 Registration period: Usually January (check www.ufs.ac.za for exact 2026 dates)
  • 📍 Campus: Bloemfontein, South Campus (Qwaqwa), or Welkom — register on your enrolled campus
  • 📄 Documents needed: Certified ID, matric certificate, proof of funding (NSFAS approval, bursary letter, or proof of self-funding)
  • 💳 Registration fee: Pay any outstanding registration fees before or during registration

🔗 Check UFS 2026 registration dates → www.ufs.ac.za

UFS Fees 2026

After receiving your UFS study offer, you will need to plan for your 2026 tuition and accommodation fees. UFS fees vary by programme and faculty:

FacultyApproximate Annual Tuition (2026)
HumanitiesR55,000 – R70,000
CommerceR60,000 – R75,000
Natural & Agricultural SciencesR65,000 – R80,000
EducationR55,000 – R68,000
LawR65,000 – R80,000
Health Sciences (Medicine)R90,000 – R120,000
Engineering, Built Environment & IT (EBEIT)R75,000 – R95,000
Theology & ReligionR55,000 – R68,000

Note: Fees are approximate and subject to annual increases. Check www.ufs.ac.za for official 2026 fee schedules.

Funding Your UFS Studies — Bursary Options 2026

If you have your UFS study offer but are concerned about fees, there are several funding options available for 2026:

  • 📋 NSFAS — UFS is a public university and fully NSFAS-eligible. Apply at nsfas.org.za. NSFAS covers tuition, accommodation, and a living allowance for qualifying students.
  • 🏛️ Free State provincial bursaries — Government bursaries for Free State students studying education, nursing, social work, and agriculture.
  • 💼 UFS merit bursaries — Available to high-achieving students. Awarded during registration or by faculty offices.
  • 🏭 Corporate bursaries — Sasol, Anglo American, Amplats, and other mining/energy companies fund UFS engineering and science students.
  • ⏰ Apply now — Many 2026 bursary deadlines are between April and June 2026.

👉 Find 2026 bursaries for UFS students →

👉 Get 24/7 AI academic support to succeed at UFS →

UFS Contact Details — Study Offer Enquiries

DepartmentContact
UFS Admissions (Bloemfontein)+27 51 401 9111
UFS Emailinfo@ufs.ac.za
UFS Student Portalwww.ufs.ac.za
UFS NSFAS Office+27 51 401 9111 (ask for Financial Aid)

⚠️ Disclaimer: StudyBuddy is an independent student support platform and is not officially affiliated with the University of the Free State (UFS). All UFS trademarks belong to their respective owners. Information provided is based on publicly available data and may change — always verify directly at www.ufs.ac.za.

faqs.question: What is a UFS study offer?
faqs.answer: A UFS study offer is the official letter from the University of the Free State (Kovsies) confirming your acceptance into a programme. It can be conditional (subject to final matric results) or unconditional (fully accepted). You access it through the UFS student portal at www.ufs.ac.za.

faqs.question: How do I access my UFS study offer at www.ufs.ac.za?
faqs.answer: Go to www.ufs.ac.za, log in to the student portal using your UFS applicant number and password, then navigate to 'Application Status'. Your study offer will appear there once it has been issued. You can download a PDF copy of the offer letter.

faqs.question: How long do I have to accept my UFS study offer?
faqs.answer: The acceptance deadline is stated in your UFS study offer letter. Typically you have 2–4 weeks to accept. Log in to www.ufs.ac.za and click 'Accept Offer'. Failing to accept before the deadline may result in your place being given to another applicant.

faqs.question: What happens after I accept my UFS study offer?
faqs.answer: After accepting your UFS study offer, you need to register for your programme. UFS registration typically takes place in January. You will need your certified ID, matric certificate, and proof of funding (NSFAS, bursary, or self-funding). Check www.ufs.ac.za for the exact 2026 registration dates.

faqs.question: Does UFS accept NSFAS funding?
faqs.answer: Yes. The University of the Free State (UFS) is a public university and fully NSFAS-eligible. If you have a UFS study offer, apply for NSFAS at nsfas.org.za to cover tuition, accommodation, and living expenses for 2026.
